description:
Closed registry of NAMED cross-field rules. JSON Schema checks the shape of a request; these check its meaning. Adding a validator requires an entry here, an independent TypeScript implementation, an independent Python implementation, a complexity bound, and a valid + nearest-invalid + adversarial-boundary vector.

No validator is expressed as an evaluable expression. The registry wires an id to a hand-written function; it never generates algorithm code from a string.

validators.description: Every quantity's unit dimension matches its declared kind, and quantities combined on one axis share a dimension. Two arrays are not comparable merely because they are the same length: a calcium concentration and a membrane potential do not belong on one numeric axis.

A stable request uses canonical unit codes. An accepted alias is REJECTED with a repair rather than silently converted, because a silent conversion changes a number without the caller ever seeing it. Adapters and `cortexel migrate` may convert aliases; normal validation may not.
Parallel arrays that describe the same observations have identical lengths. Values are never paired with times by best effort.
Declared identifiers are unique where identity must be unambiguous. A duplicate id must fail BEFORE selection, edge binding, or search can diverge on it.

Every recorded compartment series refers to a member of the declared compartment universe, and every exact ordered (compartmentId, signalId) pair is unique. Tuple identity is compared structurally rather than by delimiter concatenation, because identifiers may contain every permitted delimiter.
Bin edges are finite and strictly increasing. n edges define n-1 bins, half-open except for the documented final-edge inclusion.

An interval has start < stop, finite bounds, an explicit boundary convention, and—when the invocation declares unitDimension—a unit in that dimension. An origin-relative NEST event window additionally requires its two effective binary64 display endpoints to remain finite and strictly ordered after exact origin-plus-relative-bound accumulation. Time-window consumers set unitDimension=time, while phase-plane state domains remain valid signed intervals in their own declared units.

Event membership honors the declared start and stop closure, including NEST's open-start/closed-stop origin-relative window. Exact comparisons operate on the received binary64 quantities without an intermediate unit conversion. Under reject, outside events fail; under exclude_and_disclose they remain accepted only for the renderer to retain as table rows, count, exclude from marks, and disclose.

A NEST origin-relative event window is bound to absolute_clock time, an explicit NEST 3.9/3.10 memory/native-ms source declaration, and a digest of the exported recorder object. This verifies internal declaration consistency, not source authenticity or NEST's hidden integer-tic state.

The RECORDED sender universe is declared. Cortexel never infers population size from the senders that happened to spike — a neuron that stayed silent was still recorded, and treating it as absent inflates every per-neuron rate.

The trial universe or count is declared. Trial count is never inferred from the maximum trial id: a trial in which nothing happened is still a trial, and omitting it inflates the per-trial normalization.
A rate's recorded-sender denominator is a positive integer.

Executes exact half-open window tiling, sender-universe membership, event-count binning, complete recorded-sender denominator binding, prebinned count conservation, and count/exposure rate re-derivation through registered units.
validators.complexity: O(n log b + b) in events and bins

validators.description: count => exact non-negative integers. probability => values sum to 1 within tolerance. density => value x binWidth integrates to 1 within tolerance, and the unit is the reciprocal of the binned axis. An EMPTY histogram is never normalized into NaN or a fabricated distribution.

Forms intervals only within exact sender×trial train keys and verifies complete Cartesian train coverage, composite uniqueness, the exact half-open window, per-train max(spikeCount-1,0), total conservation, zero policy, bin membership and normalization.
validators.complexity: O(n log n + n log b) in events and bins

validators.description: Historical narrow projection of isi.within_train_only: returns only explicit zero-policy failures over the same exact composite train key. Zero intervals are never silently discarded.
A PSTH declares its alignment reference per trial, its relative window, its selected sender universe, and its trial count. Its normalization denominator is therefore verifiable rather than assumed.

The lag range is finite, symmetric about zero, and tiled by a positive width into at most 20,001 centred bins with representable outer edges. Pairwise cost is a later compiler preflight over the derived role-local event-array lengths; this validator makes no budget claim.

Each raw correlogram train owns a complete recorded-sender universe, a time for every event, a parallel sender id for every event, and—when supplied—a parallel id unique within that train. Every event sender belongs to that train's universe, including when the train aggregates multiple senders, and every event lies in the single shared observation window. Counts and duration are derived from these falsifiable inputs rather than accepted as duplicate caller assertions.
validators.complexity: O(n) in events and declared senders

validators.description: Cross-correlogram role assignment is product-shaped: reference and target are separate named containers, their train ids differ, and their complete recorded-sender universes are disjoint. A silent target remains a target because mode is never inferred from observed events. Auto input has exactly one train in both roles and excludes only that same event record's ordinal self-pair.

A pre-binned correlogram's exact pair-count length is one less than its edge length, every optional per-bin denominator is parallel, and its physical edges equal the centred lag bins declared by lagRange and bins after one registered time-unit conversion, under the published 16-binary64-epsilon relative equivalence test. Pre-binned input cannot silently carry a different axis from the one the request labels.

Revision 1 has one closed rendered product: raw_pair_count with no edge correction, or target_rate_per_reference_event with no correction or exact eligible-reference-event correction. Raw role counts come only from event arrays; pre-binned role counts are mandatory accounting carriers. Exact candidate pairs partition into counted in-range pairs, out-of-range pairs, and—only in auto mode—one excluded same-event pair per source event. A zero eligible denominator is valid only with zero pair count and compiles to null plus status undefined_zero_eligible_reference_events; it never throws or fabricates zero. Weighted and coefficient-like statistics are structurally absent.
validators.complexity: O(b) for pre-binned denominators, O(1) otherwise

validators.description: Every network figure declares a NetworkScopeV1.

The declared scope can actually support the figure's claim. Under NEST's MPI semantics a rank holds the connections whose TARGET it owns; a target-rank-local snapshot therefore supports a LOCAL in-degree but can never support a global out-degree, because the connections leaving a local source to a remote target live on the other rank. A sampled scope supports no completeness claim at all. When a figure joins two independently declared scopes in ONE request, the declared facts they share must AGREE: two scopes that disagree on snapshot time or world size describe two different networks, and drawing them together asserts a coexistence neither observed (SCOPE_MERGE_CONFLICT). Agreement is checked only between scopes present in the same request; it can never establish that separately merged ranks came from one run.

A figure whose meaning depends on isolates or zero-degree nodes declares a complete node universe. An edge list can only prove that no edge was OBSERVED; it can never prove that a node has degree zero.
Every edge endpoint is a member of the declared universe. Cortexel never silently extends a universe the caller declared complete.

Stable matrices have one Cortexel-owned target-row/source-column orientation and one exact ordered node universe. Target-rank-local matrices require a complete, unique observedTargetIds authority, every observed target belongs to the universe, and every returned connection targets an owned row; empty owned rows are observed absence and non-owned rows are not_observed. Sampled scope can prove only adjacency binary presence and its retainedConnectionCount equals the connection-row count. Adjacency accepts only sum or no_aggregation contributor policies. Weight aggregates must remain representable finite binary64, and a diverging weight map is admitted only when complete valued cell aggregates straddle its caller-declared centre.

When more than one connection maps to a matrix cell, an aggregation is declared. `no_aggregation` ASSERTS at most one connection per cell and fails if that is untrue. There is no implicit 'last edge wins' — that would silently discard a real synapse.

For delay distributions, verifies positivity plus endpoint binding, group partition, counting-policy multapse handling, exact range conservation, prebinned normalized values and scope legality; historical consumers retain the finite-positive guard for every present measurement while a structurally permitted null remains an explicitly missing carried attribute.
validators.complexity: O(e log e + e log b)

validators.description: For weight distributions, verifies exact model-comparability sets, endpoint membership, complete universes, missing-versus-zero accounting, observation-unit multapse semantics, zero-edge and log domains, prebinned conservation and scope legality; historical consumers retain the pooling guard.
validators.complexity: O(e log e + e log b)

validators.description: Verifies complete zero-inclusive node coverage, exact endpoint/policy/autapse counting, raw-connection and counted-incidence identities, one-integer-per-bin conservation, rank-local target authority and sampled-scope refusal.

Position ids equal the selected node-universe set in both directions, and optional groups form a disjoint subpartition with unique group ids, only universe members, and no repeated or overlapping node membership. Missing positions, foreign positions, and ambiguous last-wins group membership are all refused before rendering.
Both spatial axes carry the same unit dimension so an equal aspect ratio is meaningful.

Bounds are ordered and finite, lengths match the estimate, a level lies in (0,1), a sample count is positive, dispersion is non-negative, and units are compatible. An arbitrary range is never converted into a confidence or credible interval.

The supplied uncertainty variant is one this figure declares it can render truthfully. `credible_interval` is diagnostic vocabulary only in contract 1.0: every stable skill refuses it because no verified-attestation boundary exists, and structural validity alone never establishes a posterior analysis or calibration.
Duplicate timestamps in one series require an explicit policy: reject, keep_replicates, or a named aggregate. Last-write-wins is never applied, because which sample won would depend on array order.
Series overlaid on a shared axis have compatible quantity dimensions. Incompatible signals are laid out as small multiples instead of being forced onto one axis.
A vector-field sample's derivative has the dimension of its state variable per unit time, and the grid is finite and well-shaped.

Enforces the revision-2 synaptic-weight trace's complete cross-field coherence boundary: raw versus preaggregated display mode; exact model comparability and globally collision-free series/group/membership identities; positive ordered intervals; bounded finite-set exact-to-displayed time order/spacing fidelity; recording/membership continuity and lifecycle restrictions; value-after-only derived holds with complete discontinuity grids; linear-only attributable reconstructions with no discarded knots or unresolved duplicate geometry; raw-edge uncertainty fixed to none; exact shared grids and exact-unit derived pooling only where values or initial hold state can contribute; descriptive ensemble uncertainty only (SD around a mean, empirical Type-7 quantiles, or observed range), with inferential SE/confidence/credible intervals refused absent their missing design carriers; denominator, safe-cardinality and sample-count identities; and one-time weight-axis conversion fidelity across observations, uncertainty, initial states, and one- or two-sided bounds. The historical id is retained so stored validator receipts remain interpretable.

A response curve declares one of revision 2's four supported methods—mean firing rate, peak firing rate, first-spike latency, or event count—and supplies values inside that method's scientific domain. Pre-reduced voltage and generic state-variable methods are not accepted without a complete sampling and reduction basis. Rate normalization and the shared sender universe are coherent, and a peak's bin or kernel basis is mathematically identified and bound to the typed window. Raw event counts are exact non-negative integers; rates and aggregate count estimates are non-negative; a defined first-spike latency is non-negative, is referenced only to the measurement-window start in revision 2, and lies inside that exact typed window. Zero latency is the included window start; null alone means no first event. Numeric log domains are positive and unique. Raw submitted rows match the declared attempted count for every condition, and paired designs carry equal declared repeat-id sets. Aggregate effective-retained, symmetrically trimmed, undefined-excluded, and attempted counts obey the exact estimator identity and remain safe integers. Deterministic estimator underflow is refused during validation. An optional mean-rate event-count audit shares the response mask and uses the exact divisor selected by rateNormalization. Raw binned peaks require parallel exact peakBinCounts: every defined repeat rate is re-derived and raw mean, median, and trimmed-mean estimates are formed at count level before one final rate rounding. An all-null raw peak-count audit verifies only its null mask and reports derivation flags as not applicable. Aggregate binned peaks, whose repeats are omitted, must lie on the corresponding exact safe-integer estimator lattice. An uncertainty reason may not contradict these counts.
The request contains no field that only Cortexel may author. This is checked on the RAW request, before any normalization, so a forbidden field cannot be smuggled in through a default.

A caller's declared note is within its length limit and free of control, bidi-override, and zero-width characters — which could otherwise visually reorder or conceal a mandatory disclosure rendered beside it.
